inputs.label: Building Type
inputs.businessContext: Based on NFPA 13 and local fire codes; building type determines required flow density and area.

inputs.label: Hazard Classification
inputs.businessContext: Per NFPA 13, hazard classification affects design density and area of operation.

inputs.label: Sprinkler Coverage Area
inputs.businessContext: Typical coverage area per sprinkler head; varies by spacing and hazard (NFPA 13).

inputs.label: Number of Sprinklers
inputs.businessContext: Total sprinklers in the design area; used to calculate total flow demand.

inputs.label: Design Density
inputs.businessContext: Required water density per NFPA 13 based on hazard classification; typical range 0.05-0.6 gpm/sq ft.

inputs.label: Hydrant Flow Rate
inputs.businessContext: Available flow from hydrant; typical municipal supply ranges 500-2500 gpm.

inputs.label: Number of Hydrants
inputs.businessContext: Number of hydrants available for firefighting; affects total supply capacity.

inputs.label: Simultaneous Hydrant Demand
inputs.businessContext: Expected demand per hydrant during fire; typical 500-1500 gpm per hydrant.

inputs.label: Friction Loss Factor
inputs.businessContext: Friction loss in piping per 100 ft; depends on pipe material and diameter (Hazen-Williams).

inputs.label: Pipe Length
inputs.businessContext: Total length of pipe from source to farthest sprinkler/hydrant.

inputs.label: Elevation Change
inputs.businessContext: Positive if uphill; affects pressure due to elevation head (0.433 psi/ft).

inputs.label: Required Pressure at Outlet
inputs.businessContext: Minimum pressure required at sprinkler or hydrant outlet; typical 50 psi.

inputs.label: Water Supply Pressure
inputs.businessContext: Static pressure at water source; typical municipal supply 40-100 psi.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence in input data; used to adjust final results for uncertainty.

rules: If buildingType is 'storage' and hazardClassification is 'light', then warning: 'Storage buildings typically require higher hazard classification.'
If designDensity < 0.1 and hazardClassification in ['extra1','extra2'], then error: 'Design density too low for extra hazard.'
If hydrantFlowRate < simultaneousHydrantDemand, then error: 'Hydrant flow rate insufficient for simultaneous demand.'
If waterSupplyPressure < requiredPressureAtOutlet + frictionLossFactor * pipeLength / 100 + elevationChange * 0.433, then error: 'Insufficient water supply pressure.'

thresholds.flowDeficit: If totalDemand > totalSupply, then 'Flow deficit: insufficient water supply.'
thresholds.pressureDeficit: If availablePressure < requiredPressure, then 'Pressure deficit: insufficient pressure at outlet.'
thresholds.lowConfidence: If dataConfidence < 70, then 'Low data confidence; results may be unreliable.'

formulas.dataConfidenceAdjusted: overallStatus == 'Compliant' ? 'Compliant with ' + dataConfidence + '% confidence' : 'Non-compliant with ' + dataConfidence + '% confidence'

suggestedActions: If flow deficit: increase hydrant flow rate or number of hydrants, or reduce sprinkler demand by adjusting design density.
If pressure deficit: increase water supply pressure, reduce friction loss (larger pipes), or reduce elevation change.
If low confidence: verify input data with field measurements or consult local fire department.

premiumFeatures: CSV export of input and output data
Comparison with NFPA standards and local codes
